questions.question: Which company is famous for using 'Radical Transparency' to turn internal documents into viral content?

options.text: Apple
Buffer
HubSpot
Slack

questions.explanation: Buffer published their salary formulas and hiring processes, demonstrating that internal documentation is highly valuable to an outside audience.

questions.question: What is the first step in the 'Documentation-to-Content' transformation pipeline?

options.text: Wait for a professional writer to review it
Capture it: Write the internal documentation accurately for yourself/your team
Publish it immediately to social media
Anonymize the names and logos

questions.explanation: You start with your normal internal workflow (Capture). The efficiency comes from not having to 'restart' the work for the public version.

questions.question: An internal document titled 'Bug Fix LOG: Auth Timeout issue' could be transformed into which educational content title?

options.text: Our Auth system is broken again
Why we are better than the competition
How to debug authentication timeouts in [Niche] applications
Meet our development team

questions.explanation: Troubleshooting internal bugs provides the exact 'Step-by-Step' value that technical audiences search for.

questions.question: What is the 'Generous but Protective' rule?

options.text: Give away the product but protect the documentation
Share your knowledge and frameworks but protect specific customer data and sensitive trade secrets
Be generous with your time but protective of your money
Never share anything until you have a patent

questions.explanation: Expertise builds trust; sensitivity prevents liability. You build authority by being an open book about how you think and work, without compromising your competitive moat.

questions.question: Why is 'Internal Process Documentation' valuable to your ICP?

options.text: They want to build a competing business
They want to hire your employees
It demonstrates that you are a systematic expert and provides them with a 'proven' way to do something
It doesn't; only sales pitches matter

questions.explanation: When you show how you do something, you prove you actually know how to do it. It transitions you from 'someone making a claim' to 'someone showing a result'.

questions.question: What is one internal process or decision you documented this week? How could you help your ICP by sharing it? What is the one thing you would need to ANONYMIZE to make it safe to publish?
questions.promptForAI: Review if the founder understands the 'transformation' needed. Look for specificity in the 'what should I anonymize' part to ensure they are thinking about data privacy.
